source: Future Policy in Malaya. Progress in legislation for change-over to civil government__CO273:676:3
filename: Future Policy in Malaya. Progress in legislation for change-over to civil government__CO273:676:3.txt
summary:
- Tracks **British Colonial Office planning and legislative progress for transferring Malaya/Singapore from wartime military administration to civil government** (file “Future Policy. Transfer to Civil Administration. Progress of Legislation”), mainly **late 1945–early 1946**.  
- **Key actors/organizations:** Colonial Office officials **K.K./J.K. O’Connor**, **Mr Parkin**, **Mr Robert-Wray**, **W. Thorogood**; **Col. T.C. Spenser Wilkinson** (Civil Affairs, **British Military Administration (Malaya)**, HQ Kuala Lumpur); **War Office**; **Treasury**; references to **Parliamentary Counsel** and Parliament.  
- **Constitutional instruments being drafted in London** for the postwar settlement: **Straits Settlements (Repeal) Act, 1945**; **Straits Settlements Order in Council, 1946** (to fix the “appointed day”); **Singapore Colony Order in Council, 1946** (creating Singapore as a separate colony with Governor, councils, courts, and transitional provisions); **Malayan Union Order in Council, 1946** (parallel transitional provisions plus new constitutional arrangements); plus **Royal Instructions** (Singapore and Malayan Union) and **Letters Patent** revocations.  
- Major issue: **what to do about Straits Settlements Defence Regulations** expected to **lapse when the Emergency Powers Acts expire (noted as February)**; discussion of whether limited saving powers under the **Supplies and Services (Transitional Powers) Bill** are sufficient, and concern about avoiding “repugnancy” to an Imperial Act by timing any proclamation from the expiry date.  
- A **cipher telegram exchange (Dec 1945–Jan 1946)**: Wilkinson reports urgent preparation of proclamations to continue/modernize finance-related regulations and suggests similar action for wider defence regulations to avoid a legal gap; O’Connor replies (sent **3–4 Jan 1946**) agreeing in principle on timing and stating CO proposals on which regulations/proclamations to retain/amend are being drafted but **not immediately available**.  
- Other legislation flagged as needed for the changeover: an **Indemnity Ordinance** (draft completed and under consideration); implementing an **Interim Lands Directive** (approved except **Part IV on Mortgages and Charges**, linked to **debtor–creditor policy under Treasury consideration**); future measures on **debtor–creditor relations**, **companies**, and **compensation for war losses**.  
- Notes an additional Westminster bill, the **Emergency Laws (Transitional Provisions) Bill**, extending some defence regulations until **31 Dec 1947**, but described as **too limited in scope** to solve the broader problem; also mentions a proposal to **reconstitute Singapore’s Rural Board** temporarily to avoid immediate municipal ordinance changes.
